feedback.text: I am very grateful for the European Union to be interested in the protection of digital consumers. One thing I would like to be able to add to bad practices in the field of video games is the fact that companies can close video games and cancel your account ownership without consent as soon as you buy. A famous case was The crew where from night to tomorrow many users saw the game disappeared from their accounts without prior notice. The developer advertised that the game was removed in order for consumers to buy the hill out. It seems to me to be a very dangerous practice similar to what happened with iPhone and planned obsolescence and I would be grateful if it was taken into consideration when launching new laws.
